error.signature: AI tells someone in Kenya to call 911 in an emergency

verdict.summary: Kenya does not use 911. Dial 999 or 112 for emergencies; both reach police and can coordinate fire and ambulance. Ambulance provision varies, so save local hospital or private ambulance numbers too.

dead_ends.action: Dial 911 in Kenya expecting to reach emergency services
dead_ends.why_fails: 911 is not the emergency number in Kenya. 999 and 112 reach emergency services. A 911 call does not connect to local police, fire, or ambulance.
dead_ends.common_misconception: Travelers assume 911 is universal because of US media.

dead_ends.action: Rely on a single fast government ambulance number nationwide
dead_ends.why_fails: Ambulance response in Kenya often comes from private or NGO services (St John, Red Cross); 999/112 routes police and coordinates, but having a local ambulance number is safer.

workarounds.action: Save a local private/Red Cross ambulance number alongside 999/112, especially outside Nairobi.
workarounds.how: Keep the nearest hospital and a private ambulance number handy in addition to 999/112.
